title: PhD
biography: Akira Endo was a Japanese biochemist who discovered the first statin (compactin/mevastatin), leading to the development of the most prescribed class of drugs in history. Statins have prevented millions of heart attacks and strokes worldwide by lowering cholesterol.
aiSummary: Akira Endo was a biochemistry specialist who discovered the first statin drug (compactin), leading to the most prescribed drug class in history. Based in Tokyo, Japan.
techniquesInvented: Discovery of compactin (mevastatin), the first HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor (statin)

affiliations.hospitalName: Tokyo University of Agriculture and Technology

citations.title: A historical perspective on the discovery of statins
citations.journal: Proc Jpn Acad Ser B Phys Biol Sci

timeline.title: Began screening fungi for cholesterol inhibitors
timeline.description: Searched over 6,000 microbial strains for compounds that could inhibit cholesterol synthesis

timeline.title: Discovered compactin (mevastatin)
timeline.description: Isolated the first HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor from Penicillium citrinum

timeline.title: Lovastatin (Mevacor) approved by FDA
timeline.description: First commercial statin approved, based on Endo's foundational discovery

timeline.title: Lasker Award
timeline.description: Received the Lasker-DeBakey Clinical Medical Research Award for statin discovery
